title: Licence/terms snapshot capture in provenance flow
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. Provenance is part of the committed deliverable; an unprovenanced record is invalid. This automates capturing licence/terms snapshots.
objective: Add licence/terms snapshot capture (committed copy + SHA-256 + Wayback) to the provenance flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Captures a snapshot (committed copy + SHA-256 + Wayback/timestamp) of each OER licence page and each framework-terms page at record time, stored in license.snapshotRef / frameworkTerms.ref.
Snapshot is re-verifiable: the stored hash matches the committed copy; a missing snapshot blocks the record at gate-002.
Code MIT; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; the commit is DCO signed-off; no credentials or secrets committed.
